steps.title: Identify the geographic contradiction
steps.content: North American studies show a positive association (more dissolved organic carbon = more mercury), but South Korea shows the opposite relationship. This regional difference is the deciding signal.

steps.title: Match the contradiction to its implication
steps.content:
When the same variables show opposite relationships in different regions, the relationship is not universal—it is ecosystem-specific.

Choice C (‘the relationship between dissolved organic carbon and mercury reported by Berndt and Bavin reflects conditions that are characteristic of certain kinds of ecosystems in North America rather than universal conditions.’) states exactly this: the North American finding is “characteristic of certain kinds of ecosystems in North America rather than universal conditions.” The other options explain the discrepancy differently (higher mercury levels, measurement error, or precision differences), none of which address how the relationship itself varies by region.

That confirms C is correct.
